deep_analysis.executive_intro:
The FTSE screen is broadly cautious: 193 names sit on Hold, 54 on Avoid, and 72 carry insufficient data, versus just 10 Strong Buys and 21 Buys. The five highest-ranked names all score Strong Buy with full passes across cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, and risk families, but conviction is uniformly modest (~43–49%) and every signal is new (one week at signal), so the list reflects fresh quantitative flags rather than proven persistence. Sector clustering is visible—two Industrials (ME Group, Morgan Sindall) plus single names in Healthcare, Consumer Defensive, and Communication Services—suggesting value is surfacing in cyclical/industrial and income-oriented media rather than a single-theme rally. A key data limitation: earnings-quality and net-net models fail or skip on all top picks (missing OCF/NCAV), and 72 universe names lack sufficient data altogether—qualitative due diligence remains essential before sizing positions.

ME Group International (MEGP.L) — Industrials. Quantitative models flagged 14/22 passes (composite 78%, sector-relative 88%) on cheapness and quality: P/E 7.5, P/B 2.0, yield 7.4%, ROE 28.2%, D/E 28%. Passes include Graham Enterprising, Earnings Yield, Low P/E + High Yield, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Economic Moat, High Dividend Yield, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Dreman Contrarian, Composite Value, and Financial Health. Sector concentration: paired with Morgan Sindall in Industrials. Negative earnings growth (-3.9%) and FCF yield of 3.7% (below the 5% hurdle) are notable failures alongside Piotroski F-Score 4/9. Verdict: watchlist — deep value and dividend metrics are compelling, but shrinking earnings and only neutral timing (RSI 48, below 200-day MA) warrant confirmation before accumulation.

Hikma Pharmaceuticals (HIK.L) — Healthcare. Twelve of 22 models pass (composite 75%, sector-relative 91%) on P/E 11.5, P/B 1.8, yield 3.9%, ROE 16.5%, with Lynch PEG, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Composite Value, and Financial Health among the passes. Earnings growth of 23.5% supports the GARP case. However, FCF is negative (-£66.1m) and FCF yield fails at -2.0%; Economic Moat, High Dividend Yield, and Piotroski (4/9) also fail. Verdict: watchlist — valuation and growth look attractive on trailing metrics, but negative free cash flow and leverage concerns (D/E 62%, moat model failure) need resolution before accumulating.

AEP Plantations (AEP.L) — Consumer Defensive. Strongest quantitative profile in the group: 16/22 model passes (composite 87%), including Graham Defensive, Graham Enterprising, FCF Yield (11.9%), Low P/E + High Yield, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Economic Moat, High Dividend Yield, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Dreman Contrarian, Composite Value, and Financial Health. Key metrics: P/E 9.3, P/B 1.5, yield 5.9%, ROE 15.1%, D/E 0%, current ratio 8.69. No sector concentration risk among top picks. Piotroski F-Score 6/9 and Lynch PEG failure (PEG 1.24) are minor blemishes. Verdict: accumulate — broad model agreement, fortress balance sheet, and strong FCF yield support phased entry at neutral timing.

ITV (ITV.L) — Communication Services. Twelve of 22 models pass (composite 83%, sector-relative 80%) on P/E 12.4, P/B 1.5, yield 6.7%, ROE 12.3%, with FCF yield 9.0% and earnings growth 25.6%. Passes include Graham Enterprising, Earnings Yield, FCF Yield, Lynch PEG, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, High Dividend Yield, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Composite Value, and Financial Health. Uniquely among top picks, timing reads "accumulate" (RSI 28, oversold). Buffett Quality and Economic Moat fail on thin margins (6.3%); Piotroski F-Score is weakest in the cohort at 2/9. Verdict: accumulate — favourable entry timing and strong income/FCF profile outweigh moat concerns for a tactical build, with awareness of structural media/advertising cyclicality.

Morgan Sindall Group (MGNS.L) — Industrials. Eleven of 22 models pass (composite 70%, sector-relative 74%) on P/E 13.4, P/B 3.0, yield 3.3%, ROE 25.1%, D/E 18%, with FCF yield 6.1%. Passes include Graham Enterprising, FCF Yield, Lynch PEG, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Composite Value, and Financial Health. Sector concentration: second Industrials name alongside MEGP.L, adding cyclical/construction exposure. Quality models flag thin margins; Economic Moat and Earnings Yield fail; conviction is lowest in the group at 43%. Verdict: pass — construction cyclicality and the weakest composite score in the top five argue for deferring until margin quality and sector timing improve.

Qualitative risks below are not captured by the quantitative screen. Where the JSON lacks detail, that gap is noted explicitly.

ME Group International (MEGP.L): Photo-booth/vending-style industrials business may face technology disruption and venue-contract renewal risk; negative earnings growth (-3.9%) could signal structural, not cyclical, pressure. Dividend sustainability at 7.4% yield should be stress-tested against FCF conversion. Governance and geographic revenue mix are not in the payload.

Hikma Pharmaceuticals (HIK.L): Generic/specialty pharma carries regulatory (FDA/MHRA) and patent-cliff risk; negative FCF (-£66.1m) despite reported earnings growth raises working-capital or capex-intensity concerns the screen does not explain. US market exposure and pipeline concentration are unknown from this data.

AEP Plantations (AEP.L): Forestry/plantations are inherently commodity- and weather-exposed; long asset lives can mask cyclical timber-price swings. Zero reported debt is favourable, but asset-liability matching and environmental/regulatory licensing (planting, harvesting permits) are not assessed here.

ITV (ITV.L): Advertising-revenue cyclicality and structural shift from linear TV to streaming are material; Piotroski F-Score 2/9 suggests deteriorating operational trends the headline value metrics may not reflect. Pension deficit, content-spend commitments, and regulatory (Ofcom) risk are not in the JSON.

Morgan Sindall Group (MGNS.L): UK construction/order-book cyclicality, contract-risk provisioning, and thin margins (flagged by quality models) are classic sector hazards; a downturn in public-sector or housing-related work would hit earnings before multiples re-rate. Joint-venture and project-completion risk are not screened.

Data insufficiency: All five names lack research verdicts in the payload; earnings-quality models skip or fail on missing operating-cash-flow data; net-net/NCAV data is absent universe-wide. Pension, governance, and regulatory exposures cannot be scored from this file alone.

Names worth deeper research (up to 3): AEP Plantations (AEP.L) — strongest and most consistent model pass profile with clean balance sheet; ITV (ITV.L) — oversold timing plus high FCF yield merits structural/ad-cycle analysis; ME Group International (MEGP.L) — extreme cheapness (P/E 7.5, 7.4% yield) and high ROE warrant investigation into whether earnings decline is temporary or structural.

rules: Universe: only strong_buy / buy names from the latest screen.
Timing filter: names with timing_signal=wait are excluded from new buys.
Ranking: highest conviction_score first, keep at most 5 names.
Sizing: equal-weight sleeves of current NAV after exits; buys limited by remaining cash.
Costs: 3.0% applied on each buy and sell.
plan.summary: Next rebalance would target 5 equal-weight sleeve(s); leave 5 near-target holding(s).

unavailable_watch.note: Tickers marked unavailable to trade on Interactive Investor (or otherwise unactionable). Keep screening/watching; exclude from suggested trades until restored. Dashboard browser localStorage is the primary UX; this file is an optional server-side seed/mirror.
